message.content:
Research Frase.io's dashboard UI in detail. I need to understand the exact layout to recreate it for another project. Search the web for:

1. Frase.io dashboard screenshots and UI descriptions
2. The exact layout: left sidebar navigation, main content area, right sidebar panels
3. The navigation structure: what sections exist (Documents, Analytics, Settings, etc.)
4. The content editor: how it looks, toolbar, right sidebar tabs (SERP, Brief, Optimize, Questions, AI Writer)
5. The overview/dashboard page: what cards, metrics, charts are shown
6. Color scheme details: is it light theme? dark theme? what primary colors?
7. Typography and spacing patterns
8. The document list view: how projects/documents are organized
9. Any specific UI components: progress bars, score indicators, tabs, dropdowns

Search for reviews with screenshots, product tours, YouTube thumbnails, or any visual descriptions. Be very thorough - I need enough detail to build a pixel-accurate recreation.

Key URLs to check:
- https://www.frase.io/features/seo-research
- https://www.frase.io/features/seo-content-optimization
- https://searchendurance.com/frase-review/
- https://selfmademillennials.com/frase-io-review/
- https://www.demandsage.com/frase-review/
- https://juliety.com/frase-io-review/

content.text: I'll help you research Frase.io's dashboard UI in detail. Let me search for screenshots, reviews, and descriptions from various sources.

Describe the Frase.io dashboard interface, layout, UI design, navigation, and any visual details

content.content:
# Frase Content Optimization Interface

Based on the webpage content provided, I cannot describe the specific details of the content optimization editor interface, toolbar, right sidebar tabs, and layout because **the page content does not include those interface details**.

The webpage focuses on marketing messaging and feature descriptions rather than technical documentation of the editor itself. It mentions that Frase:

- "Drafts and suggests topics, headings, and questions your content should address"
- Provides "real-time" scoring and optimization guidance
- Allows users to "refine and approve" AI-generated content

However, the actual interface elements, toolbar buttons, sidebar organization, and layout structure are not described in this particular web page content.

**To get accurate interface documentation**, you would need to:
1. Access Frase's help center (referenced at docs.frase.io)
2. Review their actual product documentation
3. Explore the live application interface directly

# Frase.io Dashboard UI and Visual Design

## Dashboard Overview
When users first log in, they're directed to a dashboard area with a side navigation bar providing access to all AI and SEO tools. The interface emphasizes project management through a dedicated **Documents** page.

## Content Editor Interface
The Content Editor features what the review describes as "one of the best UIs we've seen." The layout includes:

- **Main writing window**: Functions similarly to Google Docs or Microsoft Word with formatting toolbar at the top
- **Right-side panel**: Contains AI-powered workflow tools organized into tabs
- **Research tab**: Displays competitor analysis data including headers from top-ranking articles
- **Toolbar interactions**: When text is highlighted, contextual AI options appear

## Key Visual Elements

The interface provides:
- Organized sidebar navigation for tool access
- Visual SERP analysis showing competitor metrics like "word count, topic score, domain rating, headings count"
- Folder organization system for projects
- Pop-up toolbars with AI editing options (Expand, Rewrite, etc.)

## Layout Design Philosophy
The design prioritizes workflow efficiency by placing research data and AI tools alongside the main editor, allowing users to reference optimization recommendations while writing without switching windows.

# Frase SEO Research Page Layout Analysis

## Dashboard Layout
The page presents a feature-focused design with a hero section that immediately communicates the core value proposition. The main interface screenshot shows "Research, analyze, and outline all in one interface," demonstrating an integrated workspace combining SERP analysis, question discovery, and outline generation.

## UI Components
The dashboard includes several key elements:
- **SERP Analysis section** displaying top 10 ranking articles with word count and domain authority metrics
- **Query Research panel** surfacing "People Also Ask questions" and related search terms
- **Auto-Generated Outlines** with AI-suggested headings and customizable structure
- Card-based layouts for feature presentation with icons and descriptive text

## Navigation Structure
The site header contains:
- Primary navigation linking to Platform Overview, AI Agent, AI Search Tracking, and Content Optimization
- Solutions menu (Content Marketers, SEO Teams, Agencies, Enterprises)
- Resources section with Blog, SEO + GEO Guide, and Free SEO Tools
- Call-to-action buttons for "Start Free Trial" and account login

## Color Scheme
- **Primary accent**: Forest green ("var(--forest-500)"), used for buttons, icons, and highlights
- **Backgrounds**: Cream tones ("var(--cream-50/300)") for sections and cards
- **Text**: Dark ink shades ("var(--ink-900/500)") for hierarchy
- Modern, clean aesthetic with rounded corners and subtle shadows

## Content Editor Integration
Users can "Export your outline to Frase's editor and start creating optimized content" with research remaining accessible in the sidebar during writing.

Any scale.","url":"https://www.frase.io/pricing"},{"title":"Frase Review 2026 - Is It Still Worth It?","url":"https://www.demandsage.com/frase-review/"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Frase.io's dashboard UI, though the search results don't contain specific screenshots from 2025-2026. Here's what I found:

## Frase.io Dashboard Overview

Frase got a new, simpler look at the beginning of 2024 after the 2.0 update rolled out, with most older reviews showing outdated interfaces.

### Current Dashboard Features:

When you first log in to Frase, you'll be brought to your dashboard area, from which you can access all the AI and SEO tools you need via the side navigation bar, and manage your projects from the Documents page.

After signing up for a free Frase account, you'll end up in a dashboard with three buttons to create a new document.

### Key Dashboard Elements:

After analysis is done, you'll see 4 tabs to the right of the editor window, with the SERP tab showing the "People Also Ask" section at the top and a list of the top currently ranking results, pulling the first 2 pages from Google (normally 20 results).

Users particularly like the dashboard that shows how their article stacks up to others competing for that keyword, making it easy to understand what needs to be added or subtracted to rank higher.

The search results don't provide direct links to actual dashboard screenshots from 2025-2026, but the reviews and product pages describe the interface as straightforward and intuitive with recent updates.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Frase.io's content editor interface layout and sidebar tabs:

## Sidebar Tabs Overview

There are 5 main tabs on the right-side panel in Frase, which include:

1. **Research Tab**: Frase analyzes the top 20 SERP results (Google results) for your search query and provides average word, header, link and image counts. This section also shows each of the top results, returning them as an outline with clickable content that is instantly added to the editor and cited with a link to the source.

2. **Outline Tab**: Click the headers from the SERP results in a horizontally scrollable view to add them to a list (with drag-and-drop order ability) that can be pasted into the editor as an outline.

3. **Tools Tab**: With the "In Editor" tab, you can set Background Context for the AI writer, include optimization topics, and set the tone of the "write" button in the editor. The "Tools" and "Community" section will provide AI writing tools to use (like a blog intro tool, product description tool ect.), and in the "Chat" section, you can write a command to spur an AI output.

4. **Optimize Tab**: Frase scans the top 20 search results for your query (or whichever sources you include in the list) and automatically extracts their key topics.

## Interface Design

All the tools and options you need are neatly tucked into the sidebars, making them easy to find when you need them. When it comes to writing, the workspace is uncluttered, letting you focus on the content without any distractions.

# Frase.io Dashboard Interface Overview

## Layout & Navigation

The Frase.io dashboard features an **intuitive design** where users can "intuitively guess what every feature inside the dashboard is used for without reading tutorials."

## Key UI Components

**Document Creation:**
The platform allows users to create new documents for content projects. When starting a project, you select a search type (such as "Top Google results") and initiate document creation.

**Content Structure Analysis:**
Once a document is created, "Frase.io analyzes top-ranking results and shows their content structure with H1, H2, and H3 headlines." Users can then scroll through available options and select specific paragraphs to include.

**Optimization Dashboard:**
The optimization interface displays keywords highlighted on the right side, showing "the number of times those keywords were included in the article" and comparing optimization metrics against top competitors.

## Visual Components

The review includes several screenshots demonstrating:
- The main dashboard view
- Document creation workflow
- Title generator interface
- Optimization tools panel

The author notes the interface is user-friendly enough that "anyone might" navigate features without extensive tutorials, though the optimization recommendations require SEO expertise to interpret correctly.

# Frase.io Dashboard UI & Navigation Overview

Based on the review content, here's what's described about Frase's interface:

## Dashboard Layout

The dashboard features a **straightforward, left-sidebar navigation system**. When users first log in, they land on the "Frase feed" where they can access core functionality.

## Main Navigation Structure

The left sidebar provides access to different modules, including:
- Question Ideas
- Concept Map
- Workflow options
- Content creation tools

## Editor Layout

The editor interface divides the screen into sections:
- **Left area**: Main editing workspace
- **Right panel**: "Content brief" display showing competitor research data and optimization metrics

The review notes: "On the right-hand side of the screen, you will see the content brief" with options to paste content sections directly.

## Key UI Elements

Users interact with buttons throughout the interface—"Get Started," "Create Document," and "export" buttons guide the workflow. The tool displays a "topic score from 0 to 100%" to indicate SEO optimization levels.

## Overall Assessment

The reviewer characterizes the interface as **"straightforward"** and easy to navigate, though specific screenshots aren't included in this review.

# Frase.io Dashboard Interface

Based on the provided content, I cannot offer a detailed description of the Frase.io dashboard interface, layout, UI design, navigation, or visual details.

The web page content consists primarily of minified JavaScript code, CSS stylesheets, and structured data markup for the review article itself. While the page discusses Frase.io as a tool for "outlining articles and optimizing them based on competitive research," it does not contain specific information about the platform's actual dashboard appearance or interface design.

To obtain accurate information about Frase.io's dashboard interface, I would recommend:
- Visiting Frase.io directly to explore the platform
- Reviewing official product documentation or screenshots
- Consulting detailed video tutorials or demos provided by the company
